example_code_snippets:
// C# example - Create a single-ended through-all hole
FeatureManager featureManager = (FeatureManager)model.FeatureManager;

// After creating/selecting a sketch with a hole profile point:
feature = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    0.01,  // 10mm diameter hole
    true,  // single-ended
    false, // don't flip cut direction
    false, // not used for single-ended
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,  // through-all end condition (value=1)
    0,     // ignored
    0.001, // ignored
    0.001, // ignored
    false, // no draft
    false, // ignored
    false, // ignored
    false, // ignored
    0,     // no draft angle
    0,     // ignored
    false, // ignored
    false, // ignored
    false, // ignored
    false, // ignored
    true,  // use feature scope
    true,  // auto-select bodies
    false, // not assembly feature
    false, // don't auto-select components
    false  // don't propagate
);

// VB.NET example - Create with draft angle
feature = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    0.01,                                        ' 10mm hole
    True,                                        ' single-ended
    False,                                       ' don't flip
    False,                                       ' not used
    CInt(swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll),
    0, 0.001, 0.001,
    True,                                        ' enable draft
    False,                                       ' ignored
    True,                                        ' inward draft
    False,                                       ' ignored
    0.087266462599566,                           ' 5 degrees in radians
    0, False, False, False, False,
    True, True, False, False, False
)

// To modify an existing hole's end condition TO through-all:
SimpleHoleFeatureData2 holeDef = (SimpleHoleFeatureData2)hole.GetDefinition();
holeDef.AccessSelections(model, null);
holeDef.Type = (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll;
hole.ModifyDefinition(holeDef, model, null);
holeDef.ReleaseSelectionAccess();

description: A cut revolve feature with single direction removes material from a solid body by revolving a selected sketch profile around an axis in one direction only. This is a subtractive operation that uses a single revolution angle rather than symmetrical two-directional revolution. It can include optional end conditions such as up-to surfaces or offset-from-surface, and can optionally be created as a thin-wall feature with specified thickness.

example_code_snippets:
```vb
' Example: Create a swept-cut feature with sketch profile

' Preconditions: Part must have Sketch2 (profile) and Sketch3 (path) created
Dim swApp As SldWorks.SldWorks
Dim Part As SldWorks.ModelDoc2
Dim swSweep As SldWorks.SweepFeatureData
Dim myFeature As SldWorks.Feature

Set swApp = Application.SldWorks
Set Part = swApp.ActiveDoc

' Select profile sketch and path sketch
Part.ClearSelection2 True
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0

' Create sweep-cut definition
Set swSweep = Part.FeatureManager.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' Configure sweep properties
swSweep.TwistControlType = 0                   ' Follow path
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = 0                  ' No special alignment
swSweep.CircularProfile = False                ' Use sketch profile
swSweep.Merge = True                           ' Merge in multibody
swSweep.MaintainTangency = False               ' Default tangency handling
swSweep.AdvancedSmoothing = False              ' Standard smoothing
swSweep.AlignWithEndFaces = False              ' Don't align with ends

' Create the feature
Set myFeature = Part.FeatureManager.CreateFeature(swSweep)

' Access results
Set swSweep = myFeature.GetDefinition

' Query cut sweep type
Dim cutType As Integer
cutType = swSweep.GetCutSweepOption()  ' Returns swProfileSweep (1) or swSolidSweep (2)

Debug.Print "Cut sweep feature created: " & myFeature.Name
Debug.Print "Cut type: " & cutType
Debug.Print "Twist control: " & swSweep.TwistControlType

' Release selections access
swSweep.ReleaseSelectionAccess
```

```vb
' Example: Swept-cut with guide curves and constant twist

' Select profile, path, and guide curve
Part.ClearSelection2 True
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch4", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 2, Nothing, 0  ' Guide curve

' Create sweep-cut definition
Set swSweep = Part.FeatureManager.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' Configure with constant twist
swSweep.TwistControlType = 8                   ' swTwistControlConstantTwistAlongPath
swSweep.SetTwistAngle 45.0 * 3.14159 / 180.0  ' 45 degrees in radians
swSweep.MergeSmoothFaces = True                ' Smooth guide curve surfaces
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.MaintainTangency = True                ' Merge tangent faces

' Create feature
Set myFeature = Part.FeatureManager.CreateFeature(swSweep)
```

```vb
' Example: Swept-cut with thin-wall option

Dim swSweep As SldWorks.SweepFeatureData
Set swSweep = Part.FeatureManager.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' Configure thin-wall sweep
swSweep.CircularProfile = False
swSweep.ThinFeature = True                          ' Enable thin-wall
swSweep.ThinWallType = 2                           ' swThinWallMidPlane
swSweep.SetWallThickness True, 0.005               ' 5mm wall thickness
swSweep.SetWallThickness False, 0.005              ' Same for reverse direction

' Set other properties as needed
swSweep.TwistControlType = 1                       ' swTwistControlKeepNormalConstant
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = 0

Set myFeature = Part.FeatureManager.CreateFeature(swSweep)
```

specialized_signature:
```csharp
// Creating a double-ended simple hole feature directly
Feature holeFeat = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    Dia: 0.001,                    // Hole diameter in meters (double)
    Sd: false,                     // false for double-ended (true for single-ended)
    Flip: false,                   // true to flip the direction to cut (boolean)
    Dir: false,                    // true to flip direction to extrude (boolean)
    T1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,        // First end type from swEndConditions_e
    T2: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,        // Second end type from swEndConditions_e
    D1: 0.001,                    // First end depth in meters (double)
    D2: 0.001,                    // Second end depth in meters (double)
    Dchk1: false,                 // true to allow draft angle on first end (boolean)
    Dchk2: false,                 // true to allow draft angle on second end (boolean)
    Ddir1: false,                 // For first end: true = inward, false = outward (boolean)
    Ddir2: false,                 // For second end: true = inward, false = outward (boolean)
    Dang1: 0,                     // Draft angle for first end (double, in radians)
    Dang2: 0,                     // Draft angle for second end (double, in radians)
    OffsetReverse1: false,        // For first end: offset direction away(true) or toward(false) sketch (boolean)
    OffsetReverse2: false,        // For second end: offset direction away(true) or toward(false) sketch (boolean)
    TranslateSurface1: false,     // First end: true = offset relative to surface, false = true offset (boolean)
    TranslateSurface2: false,     // Second end: true = offset relative to surface, false = true offset (boolean)
    UseFeatScope: true,           // true if feature only affects selected bodies (boolean)
    UseAutoSelect: true,          // true to automatically select all affected bodies (boolean)
    AssemblyFeatureScope: false,  // true for assembly feature scope (boolean)
    AutoSelectComponents: false,  // true to auto-select all affected components (boolean)
    PropagateFeatureToParts: false // true to propagate to components in model (boolean)
);  // Returns Feature object

// After creation via SimpleHole2, access/modify properties via GetDefinition
ISimpleHoleFeatureData2 holeData = (ISimpleHoleFeatureData2)holeFeat.GetDefinition();
holeData.AccessSelections(model, null);

// Properties available for modification:
holeData.Type = (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondUpToSurface;  // End condition type (swEndConditions_e)
holeData.Depth = 0.005;                    // Depth in meters (double, for blind holes)
holeData.SurfaceOffset = 0.0001;          // Offset from surface (double, in meters)
holeData.Diameter = 0.001;                 // Hole diameter (double, in meters)
holeData.DraftAngle = 0.017453;            // Draft angle in radians (double)
holeData.DraftWhileExtruding = true;       // Draft while extruding (boolean)
holeData.DraftOutward = false;             // Draft direction outward (boolean)
holeData.TranslateSurface = false;         // Translate surface offset (boolean)
holeData.ReverseOffset = false;            // Reverse offset direction (boolean)
holeData.ReverseDirection = false;         // Reverse cut direction (boolean)
holeData.FeatureScope = true;              // Apply feature scope in multibody (boolean)
holeData.AutoSelect = true;                // Auto-select bodies (boolean)
holeData.SetEndConditionReference(endFace); // Set reference entity (Face2, Vertex, or plane)
holeData.ReleaseSelectionAccess();

// Note: Double-ended holes use two independent end condition sets
// - First end configured via Type/Depth/Face properties and draft parameters
// - Second end would require creating separate hole or using different end condition parameters
// - Each end can have different end condition types and depths

// ignored properties (not typically set for this use case):
// - FeatureScopeBodies (managed via feature scope APIs)

```

specialized_signature:
```csharp
// Create simple hole feature with feature scope in multibody part
Feature holeFeat = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    Dia: double,                    // Hole diameter in meters (e.g., 0.001)
    Sd: bool,                       // True for single-ended, false for double-ended
    Flip: bool,                     // True to flip the direction to cut
    Dir: bool,                      // True to flip direction to extrude
    T1: swEndConditions_e,          // End condition type (swEndCondBlind=0, swEndCondThroughAll=1, 
                                    //   swEndCondThroughNext=2, swEndCondUpToSelection=10, etc.)
    T2: swEndConditions_e,          // End condition type for second end (double-ended only)
    D1: double,                     // Depth of extrusion for first end in meters
    D2: double,                     // Depth of extrusion for second end in meters
    Dchk1: bool,                    // True allows draft angle in first direction
    Dchk2: bool,                    // True allows draft angle in second direction
    Ddir1: bool,                    // True for inward draft, false for outward
    Ddir2: bool,                    // True for inward draft, false for outward
    Dang1: double,                  // Draft angle for first end in radians
    Dang2: double,                  // Draft angle for second end in radians
    OffsetReverse1: bool,           // True offsets away from sketch, false toward sketch
    OffsetReverse2: bool,           // True offsets away from sketch, false toward sketch
    TranslateSurface1: bool,        // True for offset relative to surface, false for true offset
    TranslateSurface2: bool,        // True for offset relative to surface, false for true offset
    UseFeatScope: bool,             // TRUE - feature scope active (only specified bodies affected)
    UseAutoSelect: bool,            // False to select specific bodies instead of auto-selecting
    AssemblyFeatureScope: bool,     // True if assembly feature affects only selected components
    AutoSelectComponents: bool,     // False to use selected components only
    PropagateFeatureToParts: bool   // True to propagate assembly feature to affected components
);

// Key properties via ISimpleHoleFeatureData2 interface:
ISimpleHoleFeatureData2 holeData = (ISimpleHoleFeatureData2)holeFeat.GetDefinition();
holeData.Diameter = 0.001;                      // double, in meters
holeData.Depth = 0.001;                         // double, for blind holes
holeData.Type = (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind;  // int, swEndConditions_e enum value
holeData.DraftAngle = 0.0;                      // double, in radians
holeData.DraftWhileExtruding = false;           // bool
holeData.DraftOutward = true;                   // bool, true for outward, false for inward
holeData.ReverseDirection = false;              // bool
holeData.TranslateSurface = false;              // bool
holeData.ReverseOffset = false;                 // bool
holeData.SurfaceOffset = 0.0;                   // double, offset value when using offset conditions

// Feature scope properties (multibody part specific):
holeData.FeatureScope = true;                   // bool, TRUE enables scope to specific bodies
holeData.AutoSelect = false;                    // bool, FALSE to manually select bodies
holeData.FeatureScopeBodies = selectedBodies;   // Object (array of Body2 objects)
holeData.GetFeatureScopeBodiesCount();          // Returns count of affected bodies
holeData.ISetFeatureScopeBodies(count, bodyArray);  // Set affected bodies array

// Assembly feature properties:
holeData.AssemblyFeatureScope = false;          // bool
holeData.AutoSelectComponents = false;          // bool
holeData.PropagateFeatureToParts = false;       // bool

// Ignored properties (for this specific case with feature scope):
// - ReverseOffset is ignored when end condition is not offset-based
// - SurfaceOffset is ignored when end condition is swEndCondBlind or swEndCondThroughAll
// - D2 and related second-end parameters are ignored when Sd=true (single-ended)
// - AssemblyFeatureScope and AutoSelectComponents are ignored in part documents
```
example_code_snippets:
```csharp
// Example: Create simple hole with feature scope affecting specific body
using SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks;
using SolidWorks.Interop.swconst;

ModelDoc2 swModel = (ModelDoc2)swApp.ActiveDoc;
FeatureManager swFeatMgr = swModel.FeatureManager;

// ... (select face and create sketch with hole circle geometry) ...

// Create simple hole with feature scope
Feature holeFeat = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(
    0.005,                          // Diameter: 5mm
    true,                           // Single-ended
    false,                          // Don't flip cut direction
    false,                          // Don't flip extrude direction
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,  // T1: Blind hole
    0,                              // T2: unused (single-ended)
    0.01,                           // D1: 10mm depth
    0.0,                            // D2: unused
    false,                          // No draft
    false,                          // No draft
    false, false, 0.0, 0.0,        // Draft parameters
    false, false, false, false,    // Offset parameters
    true,                           // UseFeatScope: TRUE - apply scope
    false,                          // UseAutoSelect: FALSE - manually select bodies
    false,                          // Not assembly feature
    false,                          // Not auto-selecting components
    false                           // Don't propagate
);

// Configure feature scope to affect specific bodies
ISimpleHoleFeatureData2 holeData = (ISimpleHoleFeatureData2)holeFeat.GetDefinition();
holeData.FeatureScope = true;
holeData.AutoSelect = false;

// Get the specific body to affect
Body2 targetBody = (Body2)swModel.GetBodyByName("Body1");
Body2[] bodyArray = new Body2[] { targetBody };
holeData.ISetFeatureScopeBodies(1, bodyArray);

holeFeat.ModifyDefinition(holeData, swModel, null);
```

```vbnet
Dim holeFeat As Feature = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(

```

```csharp
// Example: Create offset-from-surface hole with feature scope
Feature holeFeat = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(
    0.004,                                              // Diameter: 4mm
    true,                                               // Single-ended
    false, false,
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface, 0, // Offset from surface
    0.005,                                              // Surface offset distance
    0.0,
    false, false, false, false, 0.0, 0.0,              // No draft
    true, false,                                        // OffsetReverse1: away from sketch

    false, false, false);
```

description: This feature case documents how to create a swept-cut feature with a tool body (solid sweep). In this type of swept-cut, the cut profile is derived from a solid body (tool body) that sweeps along a path, removing material from the target body. This is distinct from sketch-based sweeps where the profile is defined by a sketch.

specialized_signature:
```csharp
// Create sweep cut feature definition
ISweepFeatureData sweepData = featureMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut);

// Configure sweep feature properties
sweepData.TangentPropagation = false;  // Boolean
sweepData.AlignWithEndFaces = true;     // Boolean
sweepData.TwistControlType = 0;         // swTwistControlType_e enum (e.g., 0=Follow Path, 1=Keep Normal Constant)
sweepData.MaintainTangency = false;     // Boolean
sweepData.AdvancedSmoothing = false;    // Boolean
sweepData.StartTangencyType = 0;        // swTangencyType_e enum (0=None, 1=Normal to Profile, 2=Direction Vector, 3=All Faces, 10=Minimum Twist)
sweepData.EndTangencyType = 0;          // swTangencyType_e enum (same as StartTangencyType)
sweepData.ThinFeature = false;          // Boolean - set to true for thin-walled cuts
sweepData.SetWallThickness(true, 0.0);  // Direction 1 thickness (if ThinFeature = true)
sweepData.SetWallThickness(false, 0.0); // Direction 2 thickness (if ThinFeature = true)
sweepData.ThinWallType = 0;             // 0=1D, 1=1DReverse, 2=MidPlane, 3=2D (only if ThinFeature = true)
sweepData.PathAlignmentType = 0;        // swTangencyType_e enum - path alignment type
sweepData.FeatureScope = true;          // Boolean - use scope in multibody part
sweepData.AutoSelect = false;           // Boolean - for tool body sweeps, set to false to control target body selection
sweepData.SetTwistAngle(0.0);           // Twist angle if TwistControlType requires it
sweepData.MergeSmoothFaces = true;      // Boolean - merge smooth faces if using guide curves
sweepData.CircularProfile = false;      // Boolean - false for tool body sweeps
sweepData.CircularProfileDiameter = 0.0; // Ignored when CircularProfile = false
sweepData.Direction = 0;                // swSweepDirection_e enum (0=Direction1, 1=Bidirectional, 2=Direction2)
sweepData.AssemblyFeatureScope = true;  // Boolean - for assembly context
sweepData.AutoSelectComponents = true;  // Boolean - for assembly context
sweepData.PropagateFeatureToParts = false; // Boolean - extend to affected parts in assembly

// After selections are made (tool body, path, target body):
IFeature sweptFeature = featureMgr.CreateFeature(sweepData);

// Ignored properties for tool body sweep:
// - Profile (set automatically from selection: tool body)
// - Path (set automatically from selection: sweep path)
// - GuideCurves (optional: can be added if needed)
// - FeatureScopeBodies (set via selection or automatically)
```

example_code_snippets:
```vb
' Example: Create a swept-cut thin-walled feature with 1D wall thickness
Dim swApp As SldWorks.SldWorks
Dim swModel As SldWorks.ModelDoc2
Dim swFeatureMgr As SldWorks.FeatureManager
Dim swSweep As SldWorks.SweepFeatureData
Dim swFeature As SldWorks.Feature

Set swApp = Application.SldWorks
Set swModel = swApp.ActiveDoc
Set swFeatureMgr = swModel.FeatureManager

' Select profile sketch (index 1) and path sketch (index 4)
swModel.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0
swModel.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0

' Create sweep-cut definition
Set swSweep = swFeatureMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' Configure for thin-walled operation
swSweep.ThinFeature = True
swSweep.ThinWallType = 0                        ' 1D wall thickness (One Direction)
swSweep.SetWallThickness True, 0.001            ' 1mm thickness in Direction 1
swSweep.SetWallThickness False, 0.0             ' No thickness in Direction 2

' Configure sweep behavior
swSweep.TwistControlType = 0                    ' Follow path
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = 0                   ' No special alignment
swSweep.CircularProfile = False                 ' Using sketch profile, not circular
swSweep.AlignWithEndFaces = True
swSweep.MaintainTangency = False
swSweep.AdvancedSmoothing = False
swSweep.StartTangencyType = 0
swSweep.EndTangencyType = 0
swSweep.SetTwistAngle 0#
swSweep.MergeSmoothFaces = True
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.FeatureScope = True
swSweep.AutoSelect = True
swSweep.AssemblyFeatureScope = True
swSweep.AutoSelectComponents = True
swSweep.PropagateFeatureToParts = False

' Create the feature
Set swFeature = swFeatureMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)

' Verify feature creation
If Not swFeature Is Nothing Then
    Debug.Print "Thin-walled sweep-cut created: " & swFeature.Name
    ' Access and verify properties
    Set swSweep = swFeature.GetDefinition
    Debug.Print "Is thin-walled: " & swSweep.IsThinFeature
    Debug.Print "Wall thickness Direction 1: " & swSweep.GetWallThickness(True) * 1000 & " mm"
    swSweep.ReleaseSelectionAccess
End If
```

example_code_snippets:
```csharp
// Example 1: Double-ended hole with 1-degree draft on first end, 2-degree draft on second end
var featureManager = (IFeatureManager)modelDoc.FeatureManager;

// Select the face where the hole will be created
// ... selection code ...

var hole = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    Dia: 0.015,              // 15mm diameter hole
    Sd: false,               // double-ended
    Flip: false,
    Dir: false,
    T1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,      // blind on first end
    T2: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,      // blind on second end
    D1: 0.020,               // 20mm depth on first end
    D2: 0.025,               // 25mm depth on second end
    Dchk1: true,             // enable draft on first end
    Dchk2: true,             // enable draft on second end
    Ddir1: false,            // outward draft on first end
    Ddir2: false,            // outward draft on second end
    Dang1: 0.0174533,        // 1 degree draft angle (0.0174533 radians)
    Dang2: 0.0349066,        // 2 degree draft angle (0.0349066 radians)
    OffsetReverse1: false,
    OffsetReverse2: false,
    TranslateSurface1: false,
    TranslateSurface2: false,
    UseFeatScope: true,
    UseAutoSelect: true,
    AssemblyFeatureScope: false,
    AutoSelectComponents: false,
    PropagateFeatureToParts: false
) as Feature;

// Example 2: Double-ended hole with different end conditions and draft angles
var hole2 = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    Dia: 0.010,
    Sd: false,               // double-ended
    Flip: false,
    Dir: false,
    T1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,          // first end: blind
    T2: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,     // second end: through all
    D1: 0.015,               // 15mm blind depth
    D2: 0.0,                 // depth ignored for through-all
    Dchk1: true,
    Dchk2: true,
    Ddir1: true,             // inward draft first end
    Ddir2: false,            // outward draft second end
    Dang1: 0.0436332,        // 2.5 degree draft
    Dang2: 0.0261799,        // 1.5 degree draft
    OffsetReverse1: false,
    OffsetReverse2: false,
    TranslateSurface1: false,
    TranslateSurface2: false,
    UseFeatScope: false,
    UseAutoSelect: true,
    AssemblyFeatureScope: false,
    AutoSelectComponents: false,
    PropagateFeatureToParts: false
) as Feature;
```

description: A simple straight hole slot created using the Hole Wizard API. This feature creates a straight rectangular slot through or into a part at specified locations. The slot is defined by its diameter, depth/length parameters, and end condition (blind or through). This use case specifically covers straight slots without counterbore or countersink features, utilizing the IFeatureManager::HoleWizard5 method with the swWzdHoleSlot generic hole type.

description: A cut revolve feature that removes material in two opposite directions from the revolve axis, useful for creating symmetric cuts around a center plane. This feature supports multiple end conditions (blind angle, mid-plane, up-to surface, offset from surface) in each direction, providing flexible control over where the cut terminates in both directions.

specialized_signature:
```csharp
// Create a cut revolve with dual direction
IFeature feature = featureManager.FeatureRevolve2(
    SingleDir: false,           // false for dual direction (symmetric around axis)
    IsSolid: true,              // true for solid revolve geometry
    IsThin: false,              // false for non-thin cut revolve
    IsCut: true,                // true to create a cut feature
    ReverseDir: false,          // true to reverse Direction 1 angle
    BothDirectionUpToSameEntity: true,  // true if up-to/offset entity is same in both dirs
    Dir1Type: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,       // swEndCondBlind (0), swEndCondMidPlane (6), swEndCondUpToSurface (4), swEndCondOffsetFromSurface (5), etc.
    Dir2Type: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,       // swEndCondBlind (0), swEndCondUpToSurface (4), swEndCondOffsetFromSurface (5)
    Dir1Angle: angleRad1,       // Angle in radians for Direction 1 (if Dir1Type == swEndCondBlind)
    Dir2Angle: angleRad2,       // Angle in radians for Direction 2 (if Dir2Type == swEndCondBlind)
    OffsetReverse1: false,      // true to reverse offset direction 1 (if Dir1Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    OffsetReverse2: false,      // true to reverse offset direction 2 (if Dir2Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    OffsetDistance1: 0.01,      // Offset distance for Direction 1 (if Dir1Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    OffsetDistance2: 0.01,      // Offset distance for Direction 2 (if Dir2Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    ThinType: 0,                // Ignored for non-thin features (IsThin == false)
    ThinThickness1: 0,          // Ignored for non-thin features (IsThin == false)
    ThinThickness2: 0,          // Ignored for non-thin features (IsThin == false)
    Merge: true,                // true to merge results in multibody part
    UseFeatScope: true,         // true to use feature scope (affects selected bodies only)
    UseAutoSelect: true         // true to auto-select all bodies; false requires Mark 1 body selections
);

// Access the feature definition via IRevolveFeatureData2
RevolveFeatureData2 revolveData = (RevolveFeatureData2)feature.GetDefinition();
revolveData.Type = (int)swRevolveType_e.swRevolveTypeTwoDirection;  // Set to two-direction revolve (2)
revolveData.ReverseDirection = false;   // Reverse the direction if needed

// Get/set revolution angles via IRevolveFeatureData2 methods
double dir1Angle = revolveData.GetRevolutionAngle(true);   // true = Direction 1, false = Direction 2
revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(true, newAngleRad);         // Set angle for Direction 1
revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(false, newAngleRad);        // Set angle for Direction 2

// Ignored properties for this use case (cut, non-thin)
// - ThinType (only relevant if IsThin == true)
// - ThinThickness1 (only relevant if IsThin == true)
// - ThinThickness2 (only relevant if IsThin == true and ThinType == swThinWallTwoDirection)
// - ThinWallType property (only relevant for thin revolve features)
```

description: A thin cut revolve feature is a specialized revolve operation that removes material from a part by rotating a sketch profile around an axis, with controlled wall thickness and multiple direction options. This feature type combines the cutting capability with thin-wall modeling, allowing precise control over the thickness of the resulting walls and the direction of material removal.

specialized_signature:
```csharp
// Create thin cut revolve feature using FeatureRevolve2
Feature feature = featureMgr.FeatureRevolve2(
    SingleDir: true,                    // true for single direction, false for dual direction
    IsSolid: false,                     // false for cut revolve (true for boss revolve)
    IsThin: true,                       // true for thin-wall feature
    IsCut: true,                        // true for cut operation (false for boss)
    ReverseDir: false,                  // true to reverse revolution direction
    BothDirectionUpToSameEntity: false, // only applies if SingleDir=false and using up-to/offset end conditions
    Dir1Type: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,        // swEndCondBlind (0), swEndCondMidPlane (6), swEndCondUpToSurface (4), swEndCondOffsetFromSurface (5)
    Dir2Type: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,        // only applies if Dir1Type != swEndCondMidPlane
    Dir1Angle: 6.28318530718,           // revolution angle in radians (e.g., 2π for 360°); only used if Dir1Type == swEndCondBlind
    Dir2Angle: 0,                       // revolution angle for direction 2 in radians; only used if Dir2Type == swEndCondBlind
    OffsetReverse1: false,              // true to reverse offset direction 1; only applies if Dir1Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    OffsetReverse2: false,              // true to reverse offset direction 2; only applies if Dir2Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    OffsetDistance1: 0.01,              // offset distance for direction 1; only applies if Dir1Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    OffsetDistance2: 0.01,              // offset distance for direction 2; only applies if Dir2Type == swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    ThinType: (int)swThinWallType_e.swThinWallOneDirection, // swThinWallOneDirection (0), swThinWallOppDirection (1), swThinWallMidPlane (2), swThinWallTwoDirection (3)
    ThinThickness1: 0.01,               // wall thickness in primary direction; if ThinWallMidPlane, thickness/2 applies to each side
    ThinThickness2: 0.01,               // wall thickness in secondary direction; only applies if ThinType == swThinWallTwoDirection
    Merge: true,                        // true to merge results into multi-body part
    UseFeatScope: true,                 // true to affect only selected bodies (false affects all bodies)
    UseAutoSelect: true                 // true to auto-select all bodies; false to manually select bodies
);

// Access and modify the feature definition
RevolveFeatureData2 revolveData = (RevolveFeatureData2)feature.GetDefinition();

// Key properties for thin cut revolves:
revolveData.Type = (int)swRevolveType_e.swRevolveTypeOneDirection;  // Set revolve type: swRevolveTypeOneDirection (0), swRevolveTypeTwoDirection (2), swRevolveTypeMidPlane (1), swRevolveTypeOneDirection360Degrees (3), swRevolveTypeMidPlane360Degrees (4), swRevolveTypeTwoDirection360Degrees (5)
revolveData.ThinWallType = (int)swThinWallType_e.swThinWallOneDirection;  // Thin wall direction type
revolveData.ReverseDirection = false;  // Reverse revolution direction
var axis = revolveData.Axis;           // IRefAxis, IEdge, or ISketchSegment

revolveData.GetWallThickness(true);    // Get thickness in forward direction (false for reverse)
revolveData.SetWallThickness(true, 0.01);  // Set thickness for direction
revolveData.GetRevolutionAngle(true);  // Get angle in direction 1 (false for direction 2)
revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(true, 6.28318530718);  // Set angle for direction

bool isBoss = revolveData.IsBossFeature();  // Returns false for cut revolves
bool isThin = revolveData.IsThinFeature();  // Returns true for thin features

// Feature scope properties (only relevant if UseFeatScope=true):
revolveData.FeatureScope = true;       // Use scope to limit to selected bodies
revolveData.AutoSelect = false;        // Don't auto-select; manually specify bodies
var scopeBodies = revolveData.FeatureScopeBodies;  // Array of bodies affected

// Properties that are NOT typically modified for thin cut revolves after creation:
// - Contours (set during selection, accessed via AccessSelections())
// - AssemblyFeatureScope
// - AutoSelectComponents
// - PropagateFeatureToParts
```
example_code_snippets:
```csharp
// Example 1: Create a simple thin cut revolve with single direction blind end condition
boolstatus = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, false, 0, null, 0);
boolstatus = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Axis1", "AXIS", 0, 0, 0, true, 16, null, 0);

Feature cutRevolve = swFeatMgr.FeatureRevolve2(
    SingleDir: true,
    IsSolid: false,
    IsThin: true,
    IsCut: true,
    ReverseDir: false,
    BothDirectionUpToSameEntity: false,
    Dir1Type: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    Dir2Type: 0,
    Dir1Angle: 3.14159265359,  // π radians = 180°
    Dir2Angle: 0,
    OffsetReverse1: false,
    OffsetReverse2: false,
    OffsetDistance1: 0,
    OffsetDistance2: 0,
    ThinType: (int)swThinWallType_e.swThinWallMidPlane,
    ThinThickness1: 0.005,      // 5mm total (2.5mm each side with midplane)
    ThinThickness2: 0,
    Merge: true,
    UseFeatScope: false,
    UseAutoSelect: true
);

RevolveFeatureData2 cutRevolveData = (RevolveFeatureData2)cutRevolve.GetDefinition();
Debug.Print("Thin wall type: " + cutRevolveData.ThinWallType.ToString());
```

```csharp
// Example 2: Create thin cut revolve with two-direction end conditions using up-to surface
```

```csharp
// Example 3: Modify existing thin cut revolve feature definition
Feature existingRevolve = /* ... get existing revolve feature ... */;
RevolveFeatureData2 revolveData = (RevolveFeatureData2)existingRevolve.GetDefinition();

// Change from mid-plane to two-direction thin wall with different thicknesses
revolveData.Type = (int)swRevolveType_e.swRevolveTypeTwoDirection;
revolveData.ThinWallType = (int)swThinWallType_e.swThinWallTwoDirection;
revolveData.SetWallThickness(true, 0.004);   // Set direction 1 thickness
revolveData.SetWallThickness(false, 0.006);  // Set direction 2 thickness
revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(true, 4.71238898039);   // 270° in direction 1
revolveData.ReverseDirection = true;

boolstatus = existingRevolve.ModifyDefinition(revolveData, swModel, null);
```

description: A lofted cut feature creates a cavity by blending through multiple cross-sectional profiles. The feature supports optional guide curves to control the shape of the loft surface and an optional centerline to define the direction of the loft. The cut is produced by removing material from the existing body.

description: A partial edge fillet applies fillet segments to portions of specified edges rather than the entire edge. This feature is useful when you want to fillet only a section of an edge, with configurable start/end conditions including distance offsets, percentage offsets, or reference-based offsets.

description: This feature case creates a revolve (rotation) feature in a multibody part that affects only specific bodies selected during creation. The feature uses IRevolveFeatureData2 interface and supports various end conditions (blind, mid-plane, up-to surface, offset from surface) in both directions, thin-wall revolves, and feature scoping to control which bodies are affected by the operation.

description: Boss/base extrusion with draft angle applied allows creating extruded features with single-ended or double-ended configurations, configurable end conditions, and draft angles applied during the extrusion process. This feature supports multiple draft directions, both inward and outward drafting, and integrates with the start and end condition system for precise control over the extrusion geometry.

description: Creates a lofted cut (cavity) feature by blending through multiple cross-sectional profiles with optional guide curves and centerline. When Close=true, the loft connects the last profile back to the first profile, creating a closed surface that is then subtracted from the solid body.
